secondary_diagnoses.value: 1) Urinary Tract Infection, 2) Known case of Thyroid disorder on treatment, 3) Grade I Fatty Liver, 4) Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis (incidental finding on USG)

hospital_course.value: Patient presented with multiple episodes of loose stools, 2-3 episodes of vomiting, fatigue for 3 days, and fever since the previous day. Initially treated at local clinic before admission. On evaluation, initial investigations showed normal CBC. Serum creatinine was elevated (1.65 mg/dL). Serum electrolytes showed low sodium (125.00 mmol/L). Urine routine showed ketone bodies (+), 10-12/hpf pus cells, 13-20/hpf epithelial cells with presence of bacteria - urine culture and sensitivity was sent (report awaited). Patient was treated with IV fluids, IV antibiotics, IV PPIs, IV antiemetics and other supportive measures. USG abdomen and pelvis showed Grade-I fatty liver changes and mildly edematous part of ascending colon up to the hepatic flexure (could represent colitis), cholelithiasis without cholecystitis, minimal ascites, and minimal right pleural effusion with underlying subsegmental lung consolidation. Repeat serum creatinine (1.17 mg/dL) normalized. TSH and Free T4 were normal. Stool routine showed 2-3/hpf RBCs and plenty/hpf pus cells. Patient was advised to stay for further management but attenders were not willing, hence discharged at request.

procedures.value: USG Abdomen and Pelvis (bedside), Arterial Blood Gas with Lactate, Urinary catheterization

discharge_medications.name: Rabeprazole (RACTIPER)
discharge_medications.change_reason: For gastric protection during acute gastroenteritis

discharge_medications.name: Thyroid medication (unspecified)

follow_up.value: Review on 09.05.2026 with CBC. Review immediately in case of fever, loose stools, vomiting, or fatigue.

pending_results.value: Urine Culture and Sensitivity - report awaited

discharge_condition.value: Hemodynamically stable. Discharged at request against medical advice to stay for further management.
